rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when referring to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Ensure all inserted annex blocks are syntactically valid AGREE constructs
Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when referring to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees in implementation annexes when referring to component ports
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
